descriptions: Brothers Keepers are in conflict with the Kang crime family (BIBO gang).
edges.evidence_quote: The Brothers Keepers are currently in conflict with the United Nations gang as well as the coalition of the Red Scorpions gang and Kang crime family, which is also known as the BIBO ( Blood In Blood Out ) gang.

descriptions: BIBO gang is allied with Red Scorpions and is a rival of Brothers Keepers.
edges.evidence_quote: rival organizations such as the Red Scorpions and their allies in the Kang crime family (BIBO gang)

descriptions: Kang group is at war with Brothers Keepers.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kang Crime Group is known to be in a war with the Brothers Keepers for control of territory in Metro Vancouver.

descriptions: Infobox lists Kang Crime Group as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kang Crime Group(BIBO gang)

descriptions: Kang group splintered from Brothers Keepers.
edges.evidence_quote: Formerly a part of the Brothers Keepers organization but due to differences between members, had splintered and formed a new organization.

descriptions: Kang crime family is listed as a rival of Redd Alert in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ASAP, Red Scorpions, United Nations (gang), Kang crime family (BIBO gang), White Boy Posse

descriptions: The United Nations lists the Kang crime family as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Kang crime family (BIBO gang)

descriptions: Jr Black Angels is a sub-clique of OVS.
edges.evidence_quote: It contains two sub-cliques known as the Jr Black Angels and Black Angels.

descriptions: Los Carajitos Locos del 19 se enfrentan al Tren de Aragua.
edges.evidence_quote: Organizaciones criminales como Los Carajitos Locos del 19 han sido las principales en enfrentarse al Tren de Aragua

descriptions: North Side Popes are allied with Imperial Gangsters.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Imperial Gangsters

descriptions: Imperial Gangsters are rivals of Latin Counts per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sureños, 12st Players, Almighty Bishops, 4GMz, SVLz, Latin Kings, Two Six, 22Boys, Almighty Ambrose, La Raza, Satan Disciples, Krazy Getdown Boys, Imperial Gangsters, Maniac Latin Disciples, Spanish Gangster Disciples, Insane Deuces, Folk Nation

descriptions: Latin Eagles allied with Imperial Gangsters as part of Almighty Folks Familia.
edges.evidence_quote: the Almighty Latin Eagles formed an alliance with the Almighty Imperial Gangsters and Simon City Royals as the Almighty Folks Familia

descriptions: Imperial Gangsters followed Latin Disciples into Folks Alliance, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: With the Latin Disciples joining Folks, the Spanish Cobras , Imperial Gangsters , and Latin Eagles immediately followed due to the United Latino Organization they had on the street.

descriptions: Simon City (predecessor) fought turf wars with Imperial Gangsters in the 1960s.
edges.evidence_quote: Simon City became involved in turf wars with other gangs including the Imperial Gangsters

descriptions: SGDs under Demon started conflicts with Imperial Gangsters.
edges.evidence_quote: Demon ... had already started conflicts with the Maniac Latin Disciples and Imperial Gangsters on the streets.

descriptions: Imperial Gangsters listed as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation, Latin Kings, Latin Counts, Spanish Cobras, Latin Eagles, Imperial Gangsters, Simon City Royals, Harrison Gents, Almighty Ambrose

descriptions: SGDs had good relationships with Imperial Gangsters through drug sales.
edges.evidence_quote: Drug sales created good relationships with all the Folks in the surrounding areas, including the ... Imperial Gangsters.

descriptions: The infobox lists Mulberry Blend as a rival of the Baxter Street Dudes.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mulberry Blend

Oficina de Envigado.description: Originally founded as an enforcement wing and debt collection service for Pablo Escobar's Medellín Cartel, Oficina de Envigado evolved into a major drug cartel and criminal organization based in Medellín and Envigado, Colombia. It controls extortion, gambling, and money laundering in the Valle de Aburrá and acts as a mediator and debt collector in drug trafficking disputes. By 2019, its activities shifted from direct drug trafficking to providing services to lower-level traffickers and mafia groups. It maintains connections with Colombian paramilitaries and guerrillas.

descriptions: The Office of Envigado is listed as a dominant paramilitary group alongside AUC successor groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2010s, the Black Eagles, Los Rastrojos, Los Urabeños, Los Paisas, Los Machos, Renacer, Los Gaitanistas, Nueva Generación, Bloque Meta, Libertadores del Vichada, the ERPAC, and The Office of Envigado comprised the dominant criminal and paramilitary organizations.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado is listed as an ally of Medellín Cartel in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Muerte a Secuestradores , Guadalajara Cartel (defunct) , The Extraditables (defunct) , La Corporación (defunct) , Oficina de Envigado , Los Priscos (defunct) , Gallón Gang (defunct) , Galician clans , Gulf Cartel , Nicaragua (alleged) , Cuba (alleged) , Panama (alleged, under Manuel Noriega )

descriptions: La Oficina de Envigado was originally an enforcement wing of the Medellín Cartel and later became its partial successor.
edges.evidence_quote: La Oficina de Envigado is believed to be a partial successor to the Medellín organization. It was founded by Don Berna as an enforcement wing for the Medellín Cartel.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado was founded by Pablo Escobar, a leader of the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Escobar's hold on Medellín further increased when he founded a criminal debt collection service known as the "Oficina de Envigado" (The Office)

descriptions: The Oficina de Envigado is a faction that remained after the Medellín Cartel was dismantled.
edges.evidence_quote: Estatus: Desarticulado (sólo quedaron facciones provenientes del Cártel como la Oficina de Envigado)

descriptions: AGC clashed with Oficina de Envigado via its armed wing.
edges.evidence_quote: Rendón's men soon began clashing with the Paisas, then a rural, armed wing of the Oficina de Envigado.

descriptions: La Oficina is listed as an opponent of AGC in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ELN , FARC dissidents , La Oficina , La Empresa , Alianza Fronteriza

descriptions: The Gulf Clan and The Office of Envigado were in conflict over the drug trade in Medellín.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2012 the Gulf Clan also got into conflict with The Office of Envigado over the drug trade in Medellín.

descriptions: Clan del Golfo had a strong rivalry with Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: La agrupación mantenía una fuerte rivalidad con otras bandas emergentes como Los Rastrojos y la Oficina de Envigado.

descriptions: Clan del Golfo shares the drug trafficking scene with Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: Comparte el escenario del narcotráfico con otras organizaciones narcoterroristas , como la Oficina de Envigado

descriptions: Both organizations operate in the same drug trafficking arena.
edges.evidence_quote: Comparte el escenario del narcotráfico con otras organizaciones narcoterroristas , como la Oficina de Envigado

descriptions: Both operate in the same drug trafficking arena.
edges.evidence_quote: Comparte el escenario del narcotráfico con otras organizaciones narcoterroristas , como la Oficina de Envigado

descriptions: El Clan del Golfo actúa en conjunto con la Oficina de Envigado para expulsar al Tren de Aragua.
edges.evidence_quote: obtener el control total del narcotráfico en conjunto con miembros de la llamada Oficina de Envigado

descriptions: A truce was established between Clan del Golfo and Oficina de Envigado in 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: un informe de inteligencia de los Estados Unidos habla de una tregua establecida por el Clan Úsuga con esta banda criminal en el año 2013

descriptions: Clan del Golfo established a truce with Oficina de Envigado in 2013 to end hostilities.
edges.evidence_quote: un informe de inteligencia de los Estados Unidos habla de una tregua establecida por el Clan Úsuga con esta banda criminal en 2013, con el objetivo de acabar el enfrentamiento entre estos grupos.

descriptions: Clan del Golfo established a truce with Oficina de Envigado in 2013 to end conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: un informe de inteligencia de los Estados Unidos habla de una tregua establecida por el Clan Úsuga con esta banda criminal en 2013, con el objetivo de acabar el enfrentamiento entre estos grupos

edges.source: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
descriptions: Oficina de Envigado is listed as an ally of ERPAC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Oficina de Envigado

descriptions: Los Caparrapos sold their franchise to Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Caparrapos habrían vendido presuntamente su franquicia en el Bajo Cauca antioqueño a la Oficina de Envigado.

descriptions: Los Paisas considered Oficina de Envigado as enemies according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Gobierno de Colombia , Clan del Golfo , Oficina de Envigado

descriptions: Los Paisas formed as a splinter from the rural militias of Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: las milicias rurales de la Oficina se separaron de la organización para conformar el grupo criminal conocido como "Los Paisas".

descriptions: A former Los Pepes member became a leader in Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: Another member of Los Pepes, Diego "Don Berna" Murillo Bejarano eventually became Inspector General of the AUC, as well as an important drug trafficker with Oficina de Envigado.

descriptions: Don Berna, miembro de Los Pepes, lideró la Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: Diego Murillo Bejarano alias «Don Berna», se convirtió con el tiempo en líder de las AUC, así como en un importante narcotraficante de la Oficina de Envigado

descriptions: The Office of Envigado is listed as an ally of Los Rastrojos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel, Barrera cartel, The Office of Envigado

descriptions: Los Zetas and The Office of Envigado are allies per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tijuana Cartel , Juárez Cartel , 'Ndrangheta , Los Mazatlecos , Barrio Azteca , MS-13 , Texas Syndicate , Triad , The Office of Envigado , La Línea , Hezbollah , Sicilian Mafia , Los Zetas Group Bravo

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado lists Cosa Nostra as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Los Zetas , Colombian paramilitary drug groups : , Los Rastrojos , Sinaloa Cartel , Moroccan mafia , Cosa Nostra

descriptions: La Oficina de Envigado affiliated with the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia to organize drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization then inherited the Medellín turf and its criminal connections in the US, Mexico, and the UK, and began to affiliate with the paramilitary United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia, organizing drug trafficking operations on their behalf.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado affiliated with AUC, organizing drug trafficking for them.
edges.evidence_quote: Murillo then began to affiliate his Oficina with the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado was initially part of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: La Oficina de Envigado, que empezó siendo parte de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC).

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado became part of the AUC as the Bloque Cacique Nutibara.
edges.evidence_quote: La Oficina comenzó a funcionar pero ahora como parte de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC) llamándose Bloque Cacique Nutibara

edges.evidence_quote: La Oficina controlaba la operación de una banda que, en su tiempo, fue uno de los grupos sicariales más temidos en el país: La Terraza

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado lists Medellín Cartel as a rival from 1992-1993.

edges.evidence_quote: The Office of Envigado (former enforcement wing for the Medellín Cartel that inherited their turf and business connections)

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado was founded as a subunit of the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: originally founded as an enforcement wing and debt collection service of Pablo Escobar 's Medellín Cartel

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado was formed from groups that served the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: agruparon los combos delincuenciales que estaban al servicio del Cartel de Medellín en una sola organización: "La Oficina de Envigado"

edges.evidence_quote: conflict with the Usuga Clan

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Clan del Golfo , Medellín Cartel (1992 - 1993)

edges.evidence_quote: problemas con el Clan del Golfo los cuales, según información de inteligencia estadounidense, fueron resueltos mediante una tregua en 2013 para acabar con el enfrentamiento entre estas dos bandas criminales

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado later formed an alliance with Clan del Golfo for illicit business.
edges.evidence_quote: fue Mesa Vallejo quien pactó la alianza con el Clan del Golfo para acabar el enfrentamiento entre estos dos grupos criminales y así establecer una relación de negocios ilícita

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado attempted to buy Los Caparrapos to avoid disappearing.
edges.evidence_quote: viéndosen obligados, al parecer, a comprar a Los Caparrapos en el Bajo Cauca para no desaparecer

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado allied with Los Pepes against Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: Murillo fell out with Escobar and eventually joined forces with Los Pepes in order to oust him.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado has alliances with Los Rastrojos.
edges.evidence_quote: it has alliances with Los Rastrojos

edges.evidence_quote: it has alliances with Los Rastrojos and Los Zetas in Mexico

edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel de Sinaloa

edges.evidence_quote: Taghi's right-hand man Saïd Razzouki took refuge in Medellín, Colombia under the protection of the Oficina de Envigado and the Clan del Golfo cartel, but was later on arrested by the Colombian authorities, and the United States' FBI and DEA.

Plug Uglies.description: The Plug Uglies were an American Nativist criminal street gang active in Baltimore, Maryland from 1854 to 1865. They were known for their involvement in election-day violence and street brawls, often using plug hats stuffed with wool as helmets. The gang participated in the 1856 Baltimore Know-Nothing Riot and the 1863 New York City Draft Riots. They were allied with the Know Nothing political party and rivaled Irish American gangs.

descriptions: Bloody Tubs were allied with Plug Uglies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Plug Uglies, Rip Raps, Nativists, Dead Rabbits

descriptions: The Bowery Boys were rivals of the Plug Uglies.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Dead Rabbits, Plug Uglies, Roach Guards, Shirt Tails, Chichesters, Tammany Hall

descriptions: Plug Uglies listed as ally of Dead Rabbits in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chichesters, Tammany Hall, Plug Uglies, Roach Guards, Mulberry Street Boys, Municipal Police, Forty Thieves, Shirt Tails, Kerryonians

descriptions: Infobox lists Bloody Tubs as allies of Plug Uglies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bloody Tubs

descriptions: Plug Uglies allied with Dead Rabbits during the 1863 Draft Riots.
edges.evidence_quote: They allied themselves with the New York City Irish Dead Rabbits gang in looting New York City during the American Civil War in the Draft Riots of 1863.

descriptions: Plug Uglies and Rip Raps jointly participated in the 1856 Baltimore election riot.
edges.evidence_quote: Together with the Rip Raps, they were also actively involved in deadly rioting at the October 1856 municipal election in Baltimore

descriptions: Cannonball members were involved in a shootout with Chosen Ones MC in 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: Nine motorcycle gang members were charged with various crimes, including attempted murder, after Cannonball members were involved in a shootout with rival bikers at the clubhouse of the Chosen Ones MC in Joensuu on 22 August 2020.

descriptions: Moraine MC was allied with Chosen Ones MC.
edges.evidence_quote: Four members of the Chosen Ones and the allied Moraine MC were also charged in the case.

descriptions: The United Nations lists the Dhak group as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Dhak group of British Columbia

'Ndrina Facchineri.description: The Facchineri are a historical 'ndrina based in Cittanova, Calabria, operating since the late 19th century. They engage in extortion, management of commercial activities, and drug trafficking, with branches in Puglia, Umbria, and Lazio. They were involved in heroin trafficking and kidnappings in the past. The group has been involved in multiple feuds, notably with the Raso-Albanese-Gullace clans, and has connections to the Banda della Magliana in Rome.

descriptions: Albanese, together with Raso and Gullace, clashed with Facchineri in the 1960s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta a Cittanova insieme ai Raso e ai Gullace si sono scontrati con i Facchineri , i Marvaso e i Monteleone.

descriptions: Facchineri and Albanese were in conflict in the Cittanova feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Cittanova : originatasi probabilmente nel 1964 quando venne ucciso un parente della 'ndrina dei Facchineri i quali risposero uccidendo un membro degli Albanese.

descriptions: Facchineri and Albanese were in conflict in the second Cittanova feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Seconda faida di Cittanova : emerge dal 1987 e termina nel 1991 con la vittoria dei Facchineri sui Raso-Albanese-Gullace.

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone were in conflict with Albanese in the 1960s faida.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta scoppia la prima faida fra i Facchineri-Varone(alleati ai Marvaso e Monteleone) e i Raso, gli Albanese , i Gullace e i De Raco.

descriptions: Avignone supported Facchineri-Varone in the 1960s faida.
edges.evidence_quote: I primi sono sostenuti anche dagli Avignone e i secondi dai Ligato- Pesce e i Piromalli .

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone were in conflict with De Raco in the 1960s faida.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta scoppia la prima faida fra i Facchineri-Varone(alleati ai Marvaso e Monteleone) e i Raso, gli Albanese , i Gullace e i De Raco.

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone were in conflict with Gullace in the 1960s faida.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta scoppia la prima faida fra i Facchineri-Varone(alleati ai Marvaso e Monteleone) e i Raso, gli Albanese , i Gullace e i De Raco.

descriptions: Facchineri were in conflict with Gullace (allied with Albanese and Raso) in the Cittanova feud.
edges.evidence_quote: La faida continuò fino al 1980 e vide la sconfitta dei Facchineri a opera degli Albanese-Raso-Gullace

descriptions: Facchineri and Gullace were in conflict in the second Cittanova feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Seconda faida di Cittanova : emerge dal 1987 e termina nel 1991 con la vittoria dei Facchineri sui Raso-Albanese-Gullace.

descriptions: Facchineri allied with Ladini in San Giorgio Morgeto.
edges.evidence_quote: A San Giorgio Morgeto i Facchineri si alleano con i Valente (Giffone)-Mercuri-Ferraro (Melicucco) e poi con i Sorbara (San Giorgio) e i Ladini (Cinquefrondi)

descriptions: Ligato-Pesce supported the rival side (Raso-Albanese-Gullace-De Raco) against Facchineri-Varone.
edges.evidence_quote: I primi sono sostenuti anche dagli Avignone e i secondi dai Ligato- Pesce e i Piromalli .

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone were allied with Marvaso in the 1960s faida.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta scoppia la prima faida fra i Facchineri-Varone(alleati ai Marvaso e Monteleone) e i Raso, gli Albanese , i Gullace e i De Raco.

descriptions: Facchineri allied with Mercuri-Ferraro in San Giorgio Morgeto.
edges.evidence_quote: A San Giorgio Morgeto i Facchineri si alleano con i Valente (Giffone)-Mercuri-Ferraro (Melicucco) e poi con i Sorbara (San Giorgio) e i Ladini (Cinquefrondi)

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone were allied with Monteleone in the 1960s faida.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta scoppia la prima faida fra i Facchineri-Varone(alleati ai Marvaso e Monteleone) e i Raso, gli Albanese , i Gullace e i De Raco.

descriptions: Piromalli supported the rival side (Raso-Albanese-Gullace-De Raco) against Facchineri-Varone.
edges.evidence_quote: I primi sono sostenuti anche dagli Avignone e i secondi dai Ligato- Pesce e i Piromalli .

descriptions: Facchineri allied with Sorbara in San Giorgio Morgeto.
edges.evidence_quote: A San Giorgio Morgeto i Facchineri si alleano con i Valente (Giffone)-Mercuri-Ferraro (Melicucco) e poi con i Sorbara (San Giorgio) e i Ladini (Cinquefrondi)

descriptions: Facchineri allied with Valente in San Giorgio Morgeto.
edges.evidence_quote: A San Giorgio Morgeto i Facchineri si alleano con i Valente (Giffone)-Mercuri-Ferraro (Melicucco) e poi con i Sorbara (San Giorgio) e i Ladini (Cinquefrondi)

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone had contacts with the Banda della Magliana through its treasurer.
edges.evidence_quote: È noto che esponenti dei Facchineri-Varone a Roma hanno avuto contatti con Enrico Nicoletti , tesoriere della Banda della Magliana .

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone were in conflict with Raso in the 1960s faida.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni sessanta scoppia la prima faida fra i Facchineri-Varone(alleati ai Marvaso e Monteleone) e i Raso, gli Albanese , i Gullace e i De Raco.

descriptions: Facchineri were in conflict with Raso (allied with Albanese and Gullace) in the Cittanova feud.
edges.evidence_quote: La faida continuò fino al 1980 e vide la sconfitta dei Facchineri a opera degli Albanese-Raso-Gullace

descriptions: Facchineri and Raso were in conflict in the second Cittanova feud, won by Facchineri.
edges.evidence_quote: Seconda faida di Cittanova : emerge dal 1987 e termina nel 1991 con la vittoria dei Facchineri sui Raso-Albanese-Gullace.

descriptions: The Fazzari family is in open conflict with the Facchineri family.
edges.evidence_quote: la pone in aperto contrasto con la famiglia Facchineri

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Facchineri.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

descriptions: Sacra Corona Libera was founded by former SCU members after conflicts with SCU leaders.
edges.evidence_quote: La Sacra Corona Libera fu fondata nel 1998 da Massimo D'Amico, Antonio Vitale e Massimo Pasimeni, criminali mesagnesi già appartenuti alla Sacra corona unita.

descriptions: Sacra Corona Libera was founded by some members of SCU.

descriptions: Vato Loko Boyz is listed as ally in infobox.

descriptions: The Aprea and Celeste-Guarino clans were in conflict in Barra.

Shui Fong.description: Shui Fong is a major Triad group based in Hong Kong, originating from a workers' union of a soft drink company in the 1930s. It operates in Hong Kong, Macau, and Chinese communities abroad, engaging in extortion, loan sharking, narcotics, and control of nightclubs and gambling dens. It is one of Hong Kong's most active triads and part of Macau's 'Four Major Gangs'. The group has been involved in violent conflicts with rivals like 14K.

descriptions: Wo On Lok is listed as a rival of 14K in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Ah Kong , Big Circle Gang , Sun Yee On , Wo On Lok , Wo Shing Wo

descriptions: Shui Fong and 14K were engaged in a gang war in Macau in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: They were involved in a high-profile gang war with perennial rivals the 14K, led in Macau by 'Broken Tooth Koi' during this period.

descriptions: The infobox lists 14K as a rival of Shui Fong.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 14K, Wo Shing Wo

descriptions: The infobox lists Wo Shing Wo as a rival of Shui Fong.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 14K, Wo Shing Wo

descriptions: A Shui Fong associate was murdered after refusing to do business with Wo Shing Wo, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: It is believed Wong was murdered after refusing to do business with the Wo Shing Wo who wanted a share of his lucrative Chinese video rental business.

descriptions: Infobox lists Wo On Lok as an ally of Sun Yee On.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Wo Hop To, Wo On Lok, Albanian mafia, Camorra, Serbian mafia, American Mafia, Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Wo On Lok members attacked Wo Shing Wo members.

descriptions: Satan's Angels merged into Hells Angels as full patch members in 1983.

Bloods.description: The Bloods are a predominantly African-American street gang founded in Los Angeles, California in 1972. They are known for their red attire and hand signs spelling 'Blood'. The gang consists of numerous subgroups called 'sets' and is engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, robbery, and pimping. They have a long-standing rivalry with the Crips and have spread across the United States and Europe.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Avenues, Mara Salvatrucha, Playboys, Santa Monica 13, Temple Street (gang), Vineland Boys, Florencia 13, Bloods, Black P. Stones (Jungles)

edges.evidence_quote: The family originated as a bootlegging organization headed by the brothers Pete and Sam Carlino

The Family.description: The Family was an African-American organized crime group based in Newark, New Jersey, founded by Akbar Pray in the early 1970s. It was primarily involved in drug trafficking, particularly cocaine, and at its height had over 300 members. The group collapsed after Pray was sentenced to life in prison in 1990, becoming fragmented.

descriptions: The Family originated as an outgrowth of the New World of Islam.
edges.evidence_quote: Pray's organization started in the early 1970s as [an outgrowth of] the New World of Islam.

descriptions: PCC lists Clan Rotela as a rival in infobox.

descriptions: Midlands Outlaws formed as a coalition against Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The various gangs united to become the Midlands Outlaws, whose 'colours' consisted of a skull wearing Native American headdress featuring seven different coloured feathers, with each colour representing one of the founding clubs.

descriptions: Midlands Outlaws patched over to become part of Outlaws MC.
edges.evidence_quote: The Midlands Outlaws later began 'prospecting' for the American Outlaws Association — the Hells Angels' biggest rival internationally — and 'patched over' to join the A.O.A. in February 2000.

descriptions: Outlaws amalgamated with Midland Outlaws to enter UK.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws expanded into the United Kingdom in February 2000 after amalgamating the Midland Outlaws, an independent club which had been in existence since 1992.

edges.evidence_quote: Notable among these are the Omar Ghazi Group, Shamali Khilafat, Gansu Hui Group, Harakat Khilafat Baluch, Jaysh ul Islam, Mullah Bakhtwar's group, the Shamsatoo group, and radical student groups in Pakistan and eastern Afghanistan.

descriptions: Karenni Army is the armed wing of KNPP.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals to the KNPP include the leftist Kayan New Land Party (KNLP) and Karenni National People's Liberation Front (KNPLF)
